methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $65M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Peak Energy, founded by Landon Mossburg and Liam O'Connor, is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised $65M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine private frontier-tech peers in the same sector and stage, supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA). The peer set includes Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M), alongside later-stage reference points including TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applied a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $65M total raised as a proxy for current valuation, implying $130M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same-stage comps yielded a median valuation of $150M, with a range of $110M to $200M. Both methods proved applicable given Peak Energy's clear positioning within the climate and energy frontier-tech segment.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $110M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $140M. The $30M spread between the stage-progression and peer-median results reflects typical variance in early-stage valuation. Key uncertainties include Peak Energy's specific technical differentiation relative to Graphyte and Fourth Power at the high end, and the degree to which public market multiples (LNZA) should inform private fair value. The peer-median method carries greater weight given the direct comparability of the three reference companies.
